communities.title: Retrieval-Augmented Generation: Methods, Robustness, and Applications
communities.summary: This community encompasses the core paradigm of Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) and its ecosystem, including foundational methods (dense retrieval, reranking, fusion approaches), robustness enhancements (adversarial defenses, fine-tuning techniques, dynamic retrieval), and diverse applications across knowledge-intensive tasks such as open-domain QA, fact-checking, legal reasoning, biomedical QA, and health misinformation detection. The community integrates large language models, retrieval mechanisms, evaluation frameworks, and datasets to address the challenge of grounding LLM generations in external knowledge sources while improving reliability and reducing hallucinations.

communities.title: Retrieval-Augmented Generation: Systems, Methods, and Applications
communities.summary: This community encompasses comprehensive research on Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG), a paradigm that enhances Large Language Models by integrating external knowledge sources to improve factuality, reduce hallucinations, and enable knowledge-intensive tasks. The papers cover architectural designs, retrieval optimization techniques, reranking strategies, multimodal approaches, evaluation methodologies, and domain-specific applications across legal, medical, financial, and scientific domains.

communities.title: Retrieval-Augmented Generation: Methods, Models, and Applications
communities.summary: This community encompasses the full spectrum of RAG research, including core retrieval techniques (dense retrieval, graph-based methods, semantic search), augmented language models (RETRO, kNN-LM, REALM), and diverse downstream applications across legal documents, financial data, code generation, and conversational QA. The entities unite around improving generative models through external knowledge retrieval, addressing hallucination, enhancing long-context understanding, and defending against adversarial attacks on retrieval systems.

communities.title: Multi-hop QA and Advanced RAG Systems
communities.summary: This community encompasses methods and resources for complex question answering tasks that require multi-step reasoning and sophisticated retrieval strategies. It unites techniques ranging from dense and sparse retrievers to adaptive pipelines, memory systems, and multi-agent reinforcement learning approaches, all evaluated on multi-hop QA datasets like HotpotQA, 2WikiMultihopQA, and MuSiQue to handle knowledge-intensive queries requiring evidence from multiple sources.

communities.title: Generative Retrieval and Ranking Methods for RAG
communities.summary: This community encompasses techniques for improving retrieval and ranking in retrieval-augmented generation systems, spanning from generative retrieval approaches that directly generate document identifiers to various reranking methods using T5 models and learning-to-rank strategies. The entities include foundational benchmarks (BEIR, KILT, TREC), core models (T5, PaLM 2), and innovative methods like Fusion-in-Decoder, GENRE, and listwise reranking approaches that optimize document retrieval quality for knowledge-intensive NLP tasks.

communities.title: RAG Systems: Retrieval, Chunking, and Robustness
communities.summary: This community encompasses methods and datasets for improving retrieval-augmented generation systems through advanced chunking strategies, graph-based retrieval paradigms, and robustness mechanisms. It addresses core RAG challenges including hallucination mitigation, document-level retrieval failures, and uncertainty quantification while covering specialized applications in financial and legal document understanding.

communities.title: RAG Systems: Retrieval, Ranking, and Trustworthiness
communities.summary: This community encompasses the core components and evaluation frameworks for retrieval-augmented generation systems, including neural retrieval methods (Dense Passage Retriever, learning-to-hash), ranking and reranking techniques (BAR-RAG, DynamicRAG), and comprehensive trustworthiness metrics (factuality, robustness, fairness, transparency, accountability, privacy). The entities span diverse RAG applications from open-domain QA and scientific document retrieval to theorem proving, evaluated across multiple benchmark datasets and integrated with large language models like GPT-4 and Llama variants.

communities.title: Retrieval Methods, Models, and Evaluation in RAG Systems
communities.summary: This community encompasses the core technical components of retrieval-augmented generation systems, including diverse retrieval approaches (dense retrievers like DPR and ColBERT, sparse methods like BM25 and SPLADE, and hybrid combinations), document reranking techniques (pointwise, pairwise, and listwise), and comprehensive evaluation frameworks. United by their focus on improving information retrieval and passage ranking within RAG pipelines, these methods and models address the fundamental challenge of identifying and ranking relevant documents to augment language model generation across various question-answering and summarization tasks.

communities.title: Retrieval-Augmented Language Models and Prompt Learning
communities.summary: This community focuses on integrating retrieval mechanisms with large language models to enhance their performance on knowledge-intensive tasks. It spans various RAG frameworks, parameter-efficient adaptation techniques, and applications across question answering, scientific discovery, and other domains where external knowledge augmentation improves LLM capabilities.

communities.title: Multimodal Embeddings and RAG Enhancement
communities.summary: This community unites research on converting vision-language models into effective embedding models through contrastive learning and adapting retrieval-augmented generation for multimodal contexts. The work spans benchmark development (MMEB, LaMP), embedding model creation (VLM2Vec, SigLIP, UniIR), and RAG enhancement techniques including context compression, personalization, and multimodal knowledge integration for improving language model performance.

communities.title: Retrieval-Augmented Generation with Neural IR
communities.summary: This community encompasses the core components and techniques of RAG systems, which combine large language models with information retrieval to answer questions by grounding responses in external documents. The entities span the full RAG pipeline—from query processing (rewriting, expansion) through retrieval and reranking to final answer generation—along with foundational neural architectures and specialized applications like policy question-answering.

communities.title: Metadata-Enhanced RAG with Hub Detection and Financial Applications
communities.summary: This community unites research on improving RAG systems through metadata-driven architectures that optimize retrieval stages (pre, semantic, and post-retrieval), combined with methods for detecting and mitigating hubness anomalies in vector databases. The work emphasizes financial domain applications with specialized datasets (FinanceBench, FiQA) and leverages multiple retrieval fusion techniques alongside monitoring tools like hubscan to ensure robust and equitable document retrieval.

communities.title: Dense Retrieval and Text Embedding Advances
communities.summary: This community focuses on developing and evaluating universal text embedding models and dense retrievers for RAG systems through benchmarking frameworks like MTEB and RAGBench. Key advances include specialized embedding models (E5, BGE-M3, Jina, Qwen3), training techniques like synthetic hard negatives and model merging, and novel architectural innovations including hyperbolic embeddings and compression-aware pooling methods that improve semantic search performance.

communities.title: Search-Based RAG with Inference-Time Reasoning
communities.summary: This community unites methods that enhance retrieval-augmented generation through test-time search mechanisms like Monte Carlo Tree Search, combining iterative retrieval-generation cycles with structured reasoning exploration. The approaches optimize both context selection (via knapsack formulations) and answer validation (via NLI rewards), enabling small-scale language models to tackle knowledge-intensive, multi-hop reasoning tasks by allocating computational resources at inference time rather than through model parameter scaling.

communities.title: Graph-Based and Multimodal RAG Architectures
communities.summary: This community encompasses structured knowledge representation methods for RAG systems, including graph data models (Labeled Property Graphs, RDF), specialized query languages (Cypher, SPARQL, Gremlin), and their application to both traditional and multimodal retrieval tasks. The entities unite around leveraging explicit graph structures and relational semantics as alternatives to embedding-based approaches, with extensions to multimodal domains for enhanced reasoning over complex, interconnected knowledge.

communities.title: Retrieval Optimization and Financial Document RAG Systems
communities.summary: This community encompasses diverse retrieval augmentation techniques and evaluation methods applied to domain-specific question answering, with particular emphasis on financial document analysis using SEC filings. The entities unite around strategies for improving RAG precision through retrieval refinement (reranking, filtering, chunk expansion), search diversification (hybrid methods, query expansion), and multimodal processing, evaluated via standard metrics and LLM-based judgment on specialized datasets.

communities.title: RAG Evaluation Metrics and Framework Systems
communities.summary: This community encompasses automated evaluation methodologies and comprehensive frameworks designed to assess RAG system performance across multiple dimensions including faithfulness, relevance, and answer quality. The entities unite around the shared goal of providing scalable, modular evaluation solutions that combine LLM-based judgment approaches, reference-free metrics, and integrated development frameworks to standardize RAG system assessment and deployment.

communities.title: Visual Document Understanding and Agentic RAG Systems
communities.summary: This community focuses on advancing retrieval-augmented generation for visually rich documents through dynamic, query-driven approaches. It unites methods that combine OCR, vision-language models, and agentic frameworks to enable intelligent region-focused retrieval, hierarchical understanding, and reinforcement-learning-enhanced reasoning over images and documents.

communities.title: Adaptive Retrieval and Evaluation in RAG Systems
communities.summary: This community focuses on dynamic retrieval mechanisms and novel evaluation approaches for RAG systems, particularly through adaptive guidance methods like ARAM and classifier-free guidance that calibrate retrieval strength using signals such as SNR and document information gain. It bridges generation model techniques (masked diffusion, contrastive decoding) with sophisticated evaluation metrics (DIG, Kendall's tau, eRAG) to optimize how retrieved documents influence language model outputs in dialogue and QA tasks.

communities.title: ChatQA: Conversational RAG with Instruction-Tuned Models
communities.summary: This community centers on the ChatQA suite of models and methods designed to enhance conversational question-answering through retrieval-augmented generation. It encompasses various model implementations (built on Llama2 and Llama3 backbones), the two-stage instruction tuning approach for better context integration, and the ChatRAG Bench evaluation framework for comprehensive RAG assessment across diverse conversational QA tasks.

communities.title: Visual Document Retrieval with Efficient Embeddings
communities.summary: This community focuses on retrieval systems that use visual inputs (screenshots) and vision-language models for document matching, employing efficient representation techniques like late interaction, quantization, and token pruning to reduce computational costs while maintaining retrieval performance. The methods combine contrastive learning objectives with compact embedding strategies to enable scalable visual document retrieval.

communities.title: Multi-modal Document Visual Question Answering and Retrieval
communities.summary: This community focuses on advancing visual question answering systems for documents through multi-modal approaches that combine text and visual understanding. It encompasses datasets, retrieval methods, and language models designed to handle complex document comprehension tasks across multiple pages and document collections, leveraging techniques like OCR, multi-modal retrievers, and agentic search-and-read workflows.

communities.title: RGB Benchmark for RAG Model Robustness Testing
communities.summary: This community focuses on evaluating large language models' performance on the RGB benchmark, which assesses robustness in retrieval-augmented generation through multiple challenging tasks. The benchmark tests critical RAG capabilities including handling noisy documents, rejecting questions lacking supporting evidence, integrating information across multiple sources, and identifying factual errors in retrieved content.

communities.title: Agentic Systems for Code Generation and Context Management
communities.summary: This community focuses on building and evaluating agentic systems that autonomously generate, execute, and refine code through sophisticated context management. The work encompasses harness optimization frameworks, benchmarking datasets like TerminalBench-2, and state-of-the-art methods for managing context in LLM-powered coding agents, with applications to challenging reasoning tasks.

communities.title: Practical RAG Implementation for Biomedical Question Answering
communities.summary: This community focuses on building end-to-end retrieval-augmented generation systems for biomedical and healthcare document question answering using AWS services. It emphasizes hybrid retrieval approaches, reranking strategies, and fine-grained evaluation methods to ensure answers are grounded in retrieved evidence and factually accurate for domain-specific applications.

communities.title: Defending RAG Systems Against Corpus Poisoning Attacks
communities.summary: This community addresses adversarial robustness in retrieval-augmented generation by developing defense mechanisms against corpus poisoning attacks that inject or manipulate passages to degrade downstream generation. The methods employ various strategies including probe-gradient instability detection, dense-retriever defenses, retrieval-stage partitioning, and content manipulation, alongside metrics for evaluating poisoning exposure and defense effectiveness.

communities.title: Multimodal Retrieval for Vision-Language Question Answering
communities.summary: This community focuses on visual question answering systems that integrate text-to-image retrieval with multimodal language models to answer knowledge-intensive questions grounded in visual content. The entities include benchmarks designed to evaluate VQA systems on mixed-modal queries and image retrieval tasks, along with foundational methods for cross-modal retrieval and visual grounding that enable these systems.

communities.title: Knowledge Integration Methods in RAG Systems
communities.summary: This community encompasses diverse techniques for incorporating retrieved information into language models, spanning both parametric approaches that modify model weights and in-context methods that augment input prompts. United by their focus on effective knowledge injection mechanisms, these methods explore different architectural and procedural pathways—from updating neural parameters to leveraging chain-of-thought reasoning—to enhance RAG system performance.
